Which of the following statements best describes the role of gravity in the formation of stars?
facts.truth: Gravity causes gases and dust particles to condense into spheres.
distractors: Gravity converts solid matter into gases and light energy.
Gravity cools gases and liquids until they become one solid mass.
Gravity pushes rocks and dust particles outward from a dense center.

At camp, Raj sees a lightning storm. He sees the lightning before he hears the thunder. What does this show?
distractors: Light and sound move at the same speed

facts.context: Aristotle taught that a force was always required to keep an object moving. Which of the following states what Newton believed?
facts.truth: An object will keep moving unless an unbalanced force stops it.
distractors: Aristotle was correct for objects on Earth but not in space.
A moving object will eventually stop even though an unbalanced force keeps pushing on it.

facts.context: The breakfast that a student ate provided energy for bicycling later in the day. Which energy transformation most likely occurred?
Which of these is the main source of the electromagnetic energy we use?
In 2001, NASA launched a probe to investigate very low-energy radiation emitted when the universe began. What part of the electromagnetic spectrum does this probe observe and record?

Francis plugged a toaster into an electric outlet. He put a piece of bread in the toaster and turned the toaster on. While the toaster was on, it changed the electrical energy from the outlet into other forms of energy. Which form of energy toasted the bread?
Which was a main force driving extensive speciation when the environment became hospitable to life after the Permian mass extinction?
distractors: increase of atmospheric oxygen to present levels
increase in genetic diversity among surviving organisms
increase in interbreeding between organisms of different species

facts.context: An object moves through space with balanced forces acting on it. Which statement best describes the speed and direction of the object as long as the forces acting on it remain balanced?
facts.truth: The speed and direction of the object will remain constant.
distractors: The speed and direction of the object will both change.
The speed will change, but the direction will remain constant.
The speed will remain constant, but the direction will change.

A force of 5 N is required to increase the speed of a box from a rate of 1.0 m/s to 3.0 m/s within 5 s along a level surface. What change would most likely require additional force to produce the same results?
facts.truth: increase the mass of the box
distractors: reduce the mass of the box
make the surfaces of the box smooth
make the surface of the floor smooth

facts.context: On which date does North America usually experience the longest period of daylight?

Food provides calories needed by the body for energy. However, the body must use energy between meals when food is not consumed. What is the most likely response of the body when more energy is used than the calories consumed provide?
facts.truth: to release energy from lipids stored throughout the body
distractors: to increase blood flow to the limbs for energy production
to transform stored proteins into carbohydrates for energy
to contract muscles to create heat energy for the body

facts.context: The motion of a car accelerating in a straight line differs from the motion of a car moving in a straight line at a constant speed. Which change best describes acceleration of a car?
facts.truth: the change in velocity divided by the time for that change
distractors: a change in the direction of the car
a change in the distance the car travels
the change in the time for the car to travel a distance

facts.context: Coal is used for generating about 53% of the electrical energy in the United States. Which of these environmental issues is directly contributed to by the burning of coal?

A 20-N force acts on a 5-kg object at rest. How fast will the object accelerate on a frictionless surface?
Which energy source is nonrenewable?
A ball rolling on the ground is observed to slow down and stop. Based on Newton's first law of motion, what is the best conclusion?
facts.truth: There must have been an outside force acting on the ball.
distractors: An object in motion eventually stops.
The ball must have been going slightly uphill.
The ball did not have enough mass to keep going.
